description:
A spatial map illustrating the geographical extent and overlap of Natura 2000 (N2K) sites and the Natura 2000 Area of Interest (N2K AOI). The map features two primary spatial layers:
1.  **Pink shaded polygons**: Represent the boundaries of Natura 2000 sites. These areas form a main elongated, irregular shape extending across the central portion of the map, with several smaller, isolated polygons visible to the top-left and bottom-left.
2.  **Dark green shaded areas with white outlines**: Represent the Natura 2000 Area of Interest (N2K AOI). This layer largely encompasses and extends beyond the main pink Natura 2000 site polygon, creating a buffer or broader area of interest. The white outlines within the green area suggest finer spatial units or a detailed underlying land cover representation.

The map shows that while a significant portion of the Natura 2000 sites (pink) are covered by the N2K AOI (dark green), there are instances where N2K site boundaries extend beyond the N2K AOI, or isolated N2K site polygons are entirely outside the main N2K AOI green area. Conversely, the N2K AOI covers areas not designated as N2K sites. No legend, scale bar, or compass is visible. The image visually supports discussions of spatial coverage dynamics of Natura 2000 areas.
